Name: Test Resource
Bio: Jonathan R. Weiss, MD, FCCP is Board Certified in Internal Medicine and Pulmonary Medicine. After graduating Dartmouth College, he received his medical degree from Hahnemann University in Philadelphia. He next completed a three-year residency in Internal Medicine at North Shore University Hospital on Long Island, NY in conjunction with Memorial Sloane Kettering cancer center in Manhattan, with both programs under the auspices of Cornell University. His final training was a three-year fellowship in pulmonary and critical care medicine at Long Island Jewish Medical Center after which he settled in Sullivan Country, NY, where he has provided medical care for over 30 years. His current focus is outpatient general internal and pulmonary medicine at the Middletown Medical office in the Shoprite Plaza of Monticello NY and nursing home care at Achieve Rehab and Nursing in Liberty, NY.
Locations: Monticello Center - Sullivan Internal Medicine Group

Name: C. S. Rametta, M.D.
Bio:
Dr. C. S. Rametta has served thousands of patients in the Hudson Valley over his long standing career, and is one of the area’s most trusted physicians. 



In addition to building a robust private practice over the last several decades, Dr. Rametta served as chairman of the Department of Medicine and chief of staff at Horton Memorial Hospital (now Orange Regional Medical Center) in Middletown, N.Y. He also served as chief of the Department of Clinics in the Department of Internal Medicine at Fort Meade, Md. while serving as a major with the United States Army.



Prior to earning his commission from the U.S. Army, Dr. Rametta earned his undergraduate degree from Seton Hall University in South Orange, N.J. and his medical degree from Georgetown University School of Medicine in Washington D.C. He completed his residency in internal medicine at the University of Virginia School of Medicine in Charlottesville, Va., where he was awarded the James Ernest Kindred Award for excellence in teaching medical students.



Dr. Rametta’s career has also included specialized experience in the field of medical quality assurance and certifications in geriatric medicine.



Board-certified in internal medicine, Dr. Rametta’s clinical interests span all aspects of preventive medicine, including comprehensive wellness evaluations, heart health, and nutrition management. Serving adults of all ages, Dr. Rametta has built his practice around state-of-the-art patient communication, medical diagnoses, and office administration.



Dr. Rametta also practices as an affiliate staff member at Orange Regional Medical Center in Middletown, N.Y.



Aside from his work as a physician, Dr. Rametta enjoys photography, gardening, Scrabble, and computer science. He is fluent in both English and Italian.
Locations: 22 Grove Street - Middletown - CS Rametta MD

Name: Kayla  Maas, PA-C
Bio:
Kayla Maas is joining Middletown Medical as a Cardiology Physician Assistant, bringing her passion for patient care and dedication to improving community health. She earned her undergraduate degree in Kinesiology from the University of Scranton and completed her master's in physician assistant studies at South University in Savannah, GA. Kayla takes a dynamic approach to medicine, focusing on compassionate care and building strong relationships with her patients.

Outside of her clinical work, she is a certified personal trainer, enjoys spending quality time with friends and family, and is always eager to learn and grow across a variety of interests. Kayla’s combination of expertise, curiosity, and enthusiasm makes her a valuable addition to the Middletown Medical team.

Name: Diane  Pine, MD
Bio: Primary Care

Name: Sandra  Binkowski, M.S.N., F.N.P-B.C.
Bio:
Sandra Binkowski is a family nurse practitioner providing care at Middletown Medical. She is board-certified by the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC).



Ms. Binkowski’s clinical experience has included cardiology, surgery, intensive care, progressive care, telemetry, oncology, pulmonology, primary care, pediatrics, and sleep medicine.



Prior to her current role, Ms. Binkowski served as a staff nurse at Orange Regional Medical Center (ORMC) in Middletown, N.Y. During her 14-year tenure with ORMC, Ms. Binkowski provided care for patients in the critical care department. She was the recipient of numerous care awards during her service at ORMC, including a three-time winner of the Shining Star award, as well as a Daisy award recipient in both 2014 and 2015. These awards are given to nurses who demonstrate excellence through their clinical expertise and extraordinary compassionate care. Ms. Binkowski has also served as an educator in the medical field, and dedicated time as an adjunct clinical instructor at Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, N.Y.



Ms. Binkowski earned her Master of Science in Nursing in the Family Nurse Practitioner Program at Dominican College in Orangeburg, N.Y. She holds a Bachelor of Science in Nursing from the State University of New York at Delhi in Delhi, N.Y. and an Associate in Applied Science from Orange Country Community College in Middletown, N.Y.



Ms. Binkowski is certified in basic life support (BLS), pediatric advanced life support (PALS), advanced cardiac life support (ACLS), and critical care nursing (CCRN). She is a member of the American Association of Critical Care Nurses, Nurse Practitioner Association of New York State, and the American Association of Nurse Practitioners. 



Ms. Binkowski is fluent in Dutch and speaks conversational German.

Name: Kyle  Goldstein, CDN
Bio:
Kyle Goldstein is a Certified Dietitian Nutritionist (CDN) with a rich and diverse educational background. He holds a bachelor’s degree in psychology, a master’s in special education, and a Master’s in Applied Clinical Nutrition. He has completed both a ten-day silent Vipassana meditation course and a 200-hour Yoga Teacher Training in Ashtanga Vinyasa Yoga in Mysore, India—deepening his commitment to holistic wellness and integrative healing.



Kyle specializes in disordered eating, unhealthy relationships with food, weight management, restorative nutrition, and supporting those navigating chronic illness. His personalized nutrition strategies are designed to meet clients where they are—fostering long-term healing and sustainable change while honoring the truth that we're all on our own journey and that progress looks different for everyone.



As a private chef, Kyle brings a creative and nourishing approach to food, helping individuals reconnect with the joy of eating. He has previous experience working in long-term care facilities, which has further informed his compassionate and practical approach. In addition to his new role at Middletown Medical, Kyle runs his own private practice, The Recovery Nutritionist, where he supports clients in rebuilding their relationship with food and their bodies through compassionate, individualized care.



Outside of work, Kyle is an avid yogi, outdoor enthusiast, and proud father. He embraces the belief that the best way to help yourself is by being of service to others.

Name: Ryan  Bigelsen, DO
Bio:
Ryan W. Bigelsen, DO is a rheumatologist who is excited to be part of the Middletown Medical team upon joining our practice in October 2024. 



Dr. Bigelsen graduated from Rowan University School of Osteopathic Medicine in 2018 before going on to complete his medical internship and residency in internal medicine at Bayonne Medical Center in New Jersey in 2022. Most recently Dr. Bigelsen finished his rheumatology fellowship at Lehigh Valley Health Network in Allentown PA in July 2024, where he gained expertise in diagnosing and treating a vast array of rheumatic conditions in both the outpatient and inpatient setting.  



Dr. Bigelsen believes in treating his patients as individuals and human beings first and foremost in order to tailor an optimal treatment plan to each patient.  He believes that by taking the time to understand an individual’s needs within the context of his or her medical condition we can optimize patient outcomes and foster a healthy doctor-patient relationship.



Dr. Bigelsen has experience in treating the spectrum of rheumatic and inflammatory conditions including but not limited to RA, osteoarthritis, SLE, gout, fibromyalgia, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, enteropathic arthritis, infectious and lyme arthritis, vasculitis, and Sjogren’s syndrome.



Some of his recent accomplishments include multiple publications and academic posters presented at regional conferences, heading a quality improvement project to improve osteoporosis outcomes, and presenting a poster at the American College of Rheumatology national conference in 2023.



He is a proud member of professional organizations including the American College of Rheumatology and the American Medical Association, and is board-certified in internal medicine.

Name: Florence  Jean-Louis, MD
Bio:
Dr. Jean-Louis brings many years of compassionate and Board Certified gynecology care to Middletown Medical. Most recently she was with Hudson Valley Healthcare Inc. in Monticello, and has also served as an Attending Physician with The Greater Hudson Valley Family Health Center in Newburgh, and Catskill Regional Medical Center in Harris.



Recognized in 2012 as one of America’s top obstetricians and gynecologists by the Consumers’ Research Council of America, among many other honors and awards she has received, Dr. Jean-Louis is a graduate of the State University of New York Upstate Medical University in Syracuse, and previously earned a B.A. in sociology from Vassar College.



Following medical school, Dr. Jean-Louis completed an Internship and Residency in Obstetrics and Gynecology with Drexel University College of Medicine/Hahnemann University Hospital in Philadelphia. She then served as an Attending Physician with Mercy Hospital of Philadelphia before returning to Hahnemann as a Clinical Instructor of Obstetrics and Gynecology. Board Certified by the American Board of Obstetrics and Gynecology (ABOG), she is also a Diplomate and Fellow with the organization.

Name: Amanda  Hansen, PA
Bio:
Amanda Hansen received her undergraduate degree with honors from Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, NY. She continued her studies at Pennsylvania’s Beaver College, where her Master’s Degree in Physician Assistant Studies was awarded with distinction. She received certification through the Physician’s Assistant National Certification Exam. During her post-graduate training Amanda worked with numerous outstanding primary and specialty physicians and medical facilities in and around the Northeast.



Amanda is a certified Emergency Medical Technician, and has received certifications from the American Heart Association in Advanced Cardiac Life Support, Advanced Pediatric Life Support and CPR Instruction. Since 1998, Amanda has volunteered her time and expertise as an EMT for the Volunteer Ambulance Corps of both Minisink and Greenville, NY. She currently serves as Assistant Clinical Instructor for Emergency Medical Technicians at the Orange Country Fire Training Center.



A proficient Urgent Care provider, Amanda is Supervisor of Clinical Staff at Middletown Medical’s Walk-In Center. Her diverse clinical experience has provided her a broad base of knowledge and insight when caring for her patients.

Name: Lee Ann  Hubbert, MSN, FNP-C
Bio:
Lee Ann Hubbert MSN, FNP-C, sees patients from age three to adult at the Ferndale Family Health. She provides a complete range of services, including proactive/preventive care, diagnosis, and treatment of acute and chronic conditions. She has two decades of experience in the medical profession. Throughout her career, she served in various nursing positions, including nine years of emergency medicine, while earning a degree as a family nurse practitioner.



She started at Sullivan Country BOCES LPN program in 2006. She continued with her RN at Sullivan Country Community College in 2011. While working nights at Garnet Health, she attended Utica College online for her BSN in 2019. She then was an HRSA grant recipient at Binghamton University, graduating with an award: Grace L. Penny Memorial 2023.



She practiced as a registered nurse from 2012-2023, employed at Garnet Health at the Callicoon and Catskill campus. During her time at the Callicoon location, she provided direct patient care in the critical access emergency department and swing bed program and assisted with case management.



While at Garnet Health Harris campus, she started her RN career on the mental health/detox unit, then transferred to the medical/surgical unit for several years of experience. She then joined the team in the emergency department.



Certifications obtained basic life support (BLS), pediatric life support (PALS), advanced life support (ALS), Trauma Nurse Core Course Certification (TNCC), and emergency nurse pediatric course (ENPC). She was named Employee of the Year 2022 at Garnet Health. She was awarded Saving of a Life in 2017.



As an LPN, she worked at several locations, such as Achieve, where she obtained her long-term care experience. The Center for Discovery on the CSA Farm as a direct support professional (DSP). She worked at Garnet Health in the doctors' office but mainly at the Women's Health Center in Monticello office.



She was a certified nursing assistant for one year.



Lee Ann is local to Sullivan Country. She lives and loves this area. She and her husband, Jim are busy raising their four children. She believes Sullivan Country deserves health and hopes to provide excellent health care locally.

Name: Andrew  Overton, DPT
Bio:
Andrew Overton, DPT is a dedicated physical therapy professional who specializes in the treatment and rehabilitation of sports-related injuries from recreational, amateur, and professional sports.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Andrew served as a physical therapist with Access Physical Therapy and Wellness in Chester, N.Y. where he evaluated and treated a variety of orthopedic conditions. Throughout his career, Andrew has also worked with patients at Falmouth Hospital in Falmouth, Mass., Baystate Outpatient Physical Therapy in Springfield, Mass., Central Massachusetts Physical Therapy in West Boylston, Mass., Shriners Children Hospital in Springfield, Mass., and Pine Bush Physical Therapy in Pine Bush, N.Y.



Andrew earned his Bachelor of Health Science and his Doctorate of Physical Therapy from Springfield College in Springfield, Mass. where he graduated magna cum laude.



When he is not spending time with patients, Andrew is passionate about the outdoors and frequently spends time hiking or cycling. Additionally, he is a competitive CrossFit athlete with a background in Olympic weightlifting.



Andrew is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Migdalia  Colon, MHC-LP
Bio:
My name is Migdalia Colon and I bring over 26 years of experience working with children, adolescents, and families, guiding them on their path to emotional well-being. I am deeply passionate about helping individuals and families reconnect and find healing, especially in my work with at-risk youth. In my past experiences, I have worked in Community Health, in the agency setting, as a care/case manager, and community clinical specialist, performing home based behavioral talk therapy.  I have in the past also worked with patients diagnosed with substance use disorder, and those with dual diagnosis, as well as Early Intervention (EI). My approach is rooted in empathy, understanding the unique challenges each person faces, and working collaboratively to foster healthy, transformative change. Educationally, I hold a Bachelor of Science in Psychology from Mercy University and a Master of Science in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Capella University.



As I join the Middletown Medical Behavioral Health team, I’m excited to contribute my expertise in individual and group counseling, specializing in anxiety, depression, and trauma-informed care. Together, we will work on the areas you wish to address, creating meaningful and empowering narratives that support your overall mental health and wellness.

Name: Tedhar  Setton, MD
Bio: Urgent Care

Name: Prajith  Mepparambath, MD
Bio:
Prajith Mepparambath, M.D.

PEDIATRIC ENDOCRINOLOGY



Dr. Prajith Mepparambath is a pediatric endocrinologist specializing in the acute and advanced care of pediatric endocrine patients, including those patients with diabetes, growth disorders, thyroid disorders, adrenal disorders, as well as various other endocrine disorders.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Mepparambath was providing care to pediatric patients with various endocrine disorders at New York University Winthrop Hospital in Mineola, N.Y. He also previously provided care as a pediatric hospitalist at Northwell Health in New York, N.Y. In addition to his experience as a medical provider, Dr. Mepparambath has also invested his time to the field of medical research. He has contributed to various research projects at the New York University Winthrop Hospital in Mineola, N.Y. as well as to quality improvement projects at the John H. Stroger, Jr. Hospital of Cook Country in Chicago, Ill.



Dr. Mepparambath has also contributed his expertise to medical education through speaking engagements and mentoring residents and medical students. His efforts were recognized in April 2018 when he was awarded a Poster of Distinction by New York University Winthrop Hospital in Mineola, N.Y. He was invited to present his research project at the Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) Meeting in Toronto, Canada in May 2018.



Dr. Mepparambath is also passionate about community outreach and advocacy, and has participated in seminars at local high schools, as well as at various health centers, including Misericordia Heart of Mercy in Chicago, IL. and Maryville Children's Healthcare Center in Chicago, IL.



Dr. Mepparambath earned both his Advanced Post Graduate Medical Diploma in Child Health and his Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) at the Government Medical College in Calicut, India. He completed pediatric residencies at the Calicut Medical College in Calicut, India, and the John H. Stroger, Jr. Hospital of Cook Country in Chicago, IL. Dr. Mepparambath completed his pediatric endocrinology fellowship at New York University Winthrop Hospital in Mineola, N.Y.



Dr. Mepparambath holds certifications in Basic Life Support (BLS) and Advanced Pediatric Life Support (PALS). Dr. Mepparambath is board-certified in pediatrics and he is also a member of the American Academy of Pediatrics, the American Medical Association, and the Endocrine Society.



Dr. Mepparambath speaks English, Malayalam, Hindi, and basic Spanish.

Name: Danielle  Long, NP-C
Bio:
Danielle Long, NP-C, is a Family Nurse Practitioner with nearly two decades of healthcare experience delivering compassionate, impactful care. Since starting her nursing journey in 2006, Danielle has excelled in diverse settings—long-term care, nursing management, medical-surgical nursing, pediatrics, and critical care—where her passion for understanding human behavior and improving health outcomes took root. 

In 2016, while working in intensive care units, Danielle observed how preventable conditions often escalated due to limited healthcare access and education, particularly in rural communities. This insight sparked her mission to bridge gaps in care. She earned her Master of Science in Nursing as a Family Nurse Practitioner from Binghamton University in 2021, graduating Summa Cum Laude with a focus on rural healthcare and preventive medicine. 

As a lifelong Sullivan Country resident and local mother of three, Danielle deeply understands the unique challenges her community faces. Now at Middletown Medical, where she joined in 2023, she blends her clinical experience and knowledge with behavioral science principles to empower patients. By decoding the “why” behind health choices, Danielle designs personalized strategies that inspire lasting change—whether helping families prevent chronic conditions or guiding individuals toward healthier lifestyles. 

Danielle’s warm, patient-centered approach makes her a trusted partner in transforming health outcomes for our community. She is excited to provide care for patients 2 years and older in both our Ferndale and Ellenville offices. When she is not caring for patients, you may find her at her favorite coffee shops, watching stand-up comedy, hiking, spending time with her children and family, and smiling. Let’s connect today!

Name: Donyelle  Echevarria, MSN, FNP-BC
Bio:
Donyelle Echevarria MSN, FNP-BC, is a board-certified Family Nurse Practitioner, providing compassionate care in Middletown Medical Urgent Care. Ms. Echevarria focuses on inclusivity, evidence-based practice, and comprehensive care . Ms. Echevarria is board certified with both the American Association of Nurse Practitioners (AANP) & the American Nurse Credentialing Center (ANCC).



Ms. Echevarria has 25 years of clinical experience in the fields of: Emergency Medicine, Trauma, Surgical ICU stepdown, Telemetry and Med-Surg. She has most previously worked at Garnet Emergency Department in Middletown, NY and Good Samaritan Hospital Emergency Department in Suffern, NY.  Ms. Echevarria is a Clinical Nursing Instructor at SUNY Orange in Middletown, NY.  Ms. Echevarria also works per diem at Bon Secours Community Hospital Emergency Department in Port Jervis, NY and St. Anthony's Community Hospital Emergency Department in Warwick, NY as an Emergency Medicine NP.



Ms. Echevarria earned her Master of Science in Nursing- Family Nurse Practitioner from Dominican University. She earned her Bachelor of Science in Nursing at SUNY Delhi, Bachelor of Science in Human Services at Old Dominion University and her Associate of Applied Science in Nursing at SUNY Orange.



Her certifications include, TNCC, ACLS, PALS and BLS. She is a member of the American Association of Nurse Practitioners, the American Nurses Association, New York State Nurses Association, and Sigma Theta Tau, International Honor Society of Nursing - Chi Tau Chapter.



In her free time, she enjoys spending time with her family, participating in and watching sports, and traveling.

Name: Christine  Quinlan, FNP
Bio:
Christine Quinlan is a graduate of Dominican College in Orangeburg New York, where she earned her Master of Science Degree in Nursing. She has had extensive training and experience in all areas of nursing and trauma care, beginning with many years of experience at St. Luke’s Cornwall Hospital Emergency Room. She worked in the Good Samaritan Hospital Emergency Room in Suffern, NY as well. In 2005, she was nominated for the Rockland Country Nurse of the Year award.



A certified Family Nurse Practitioner, Christine has had additional post-graduate training in Trauma Nursing. During her career in the nursing profession, Christine has developed strong communication skills with her patients and with attending physicians and staff, with the belief that open lines of communication allow for better care. She is adept and thorough in her assessment and management of acute and chronic illnesses, with a clear initiative to always monitor the wellbeing of her patients.

Name: Jonathan.  Weiss, MD, FCCP
Bio: Jonathan R. Weiss, MD, FCCP is Board Certified in Internal Medicine and Pulmonary Medicine. After graduating Dartmouth College, he received his medical degree from Hahnemann University in Philadelphia. He next completed a three-year residency in Internal Medicine at North Shore University Hospital on Long Island, NY in conjunction with Memorial Sloane Kettering cancer center in Manhattan, with both programs under the auspices of Cornell University. His final training was a three-year fellowship in pulmonary and critical care medicine at Long Island Jewish Medical Center after which he settled in Sullivan Country, NY, where he has provided medical care for over 30 years. His current focus is outpatient general internal and pulmonary medicine at the Middletown Medical office in the Shoprite Plaza of Monticello NY and nursing home care at Achieve Rehab and Nursing in Liberty, NY.
Locations: Monticello Center - Sullivan Internal Medicine Group

Name: Mukesh  Jhaveri, MD Neuropsychiatry
Bio: office located at 24 Grove Street Middletown NY
Locations: 22 Grove Street - Middletown - CS Rametta MD

Name: Maryann  Park, MD
Bio:
Prior to earning her medical degree from the University of Medicine & Dentistry of New Jersey, Dr. Park graduated with honors from Smith College in Massachusetts. She completed her internship and residency in Internal Medicine at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine/Montefiore Medical Center in Bronx, New York, where she received an award for Outstanding Resident.



After completing her fellowship in Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine at New York University/Bellvue Hospital, she held positions at the National Institutes of Health and at Prince George’s Hospital Center Level 1 Trauma Center, both in Maryland. Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Park practiced for many years at the VA New Jersey Healthcare System, where she served as Chief of Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine as well as Chief of the Medical Intensive Care Unit. She has been nominated for ‘Top Doctor’ in the Hudson Valley.



An accomplished medical researcher, Dr. Park has contributed to many medical journal publications, completed multiple clinical trials and has lectured extensively in her field.



Dr. Park is highly skilled in treating a wide range of lung diseases and has particular interest in pain management and the care of critically ill patients.



She has been nominated for ‘Top Doctor’ in the Hudson Valley.

Name: Uri  Napchan, MD
Bio:
Uri Napchan, M.D., Director, is a board certified neurologist who is also board certified in pain medicine with a sub-specialty in headache medicine. He most recently practiced at the Montefiore Medical Center’s Headache Center, the first of its kind in the world.



He completed his residency at Drexel University College of Medicine, Philadelphia, PA and a fellowship in pain medicine at Louisiana State University Health Sciences Center. He completed additional fellowships in headache medicine and neuroscience at Montefiore Medical Center, Bronx, NY. He was also an Assistant Professor of Neurology at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine, Bronx, NY. He is a guest lecturer on topics in headache medicine as well as having published numerous articles and studies. Dr. Napchan is trilingual in English, Spanish and Portuguese.



I look forward to helping those with chronic, severe headaches and migraines find relief. I approach every patient individually and tailor a specific plan to help alleviate their pain.”, states Dr. Napchan.
Certifications: Board-Certified in Pain Medicine with a Sub-Specialty in Headache Medicine

Name: Steven  Weinstein, MD
Bio:
Steven Weinstein, M.D. is triple board certified in Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation, Pain

Medicine and Electrodiagnostic Medicine (EMG/NCS). He received his Bachelor of Science in

Economics from the University of Pennsylvania. He earned his Doctor of Medicine at State

University of New York Health Science Center at Brooklyn.



Dr. Weinstein completed his internship at Northwestern University at the McGaw Medical

Center and a residency in Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation at the prestigious

Rehabilitation Institute of Chicago.

Dr. Weinstein is a member of the American Academy of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation,

North American Spine Society and Medical Society of the State of New York.



Dr. Weinstein brings his wealth of knowledge and understanding of physical medicine and

rehabilitation, pain management and EMG to our practice.



Dr. Weinstein can treat conditions such as:

       - Pain Management

       - Chronic Back Pain

       - Chronic Neck Pain

       - Epidural Injections/Hip Injections

       - Non-surgical orthopedics – shoulder, elbow, hand, hip, knee, ankle

       - EMG/NCS

Name: Gena  Brower, FNP
Bio:
Gena Brower is a Board Certified Family Nurse Practitioner with a wide range of experience in many different settings. She comes to Middletown Medical from Lenox Hill Hospital in New York, where she was a Registered Nurse in the Emergency Department, while also serving as a member of the North Shore Long Island Jewish Health System Sepsis Task Force.  During this time, she was also on rotations with various entities for Adult & Geriatric, Women’s Health and Pediatrics.



Previously, Ms. Brower was an Emergency Room R.N. with Winthrop University Hospital in Mineola, N.Y., as well as a Burn ICU Nursing Extern with Weill Cornell Hospital in New York.



A graduate of Hunter Bellevue School of Nursing in New York, N.Y., where he earned a B.S. in Nursing, Ms. Brower went on to earn an M.S. in Nursing from Pace University in New York.

Name: Sagari  Murali, NP
Bio: Nephrology

Name: Barbara  Hearle, FNP
Bio:
Barbara Lynn Hearle, FNP is a certified family nurse practitioner providing compassionate and comprehensive care to patients within the Urology Division of Middletown Medical, PC. She is certified through the American Academy of Nurse Practitioners.



Prior to her current role, Mrs. Hearle served patients with Advocare Pediatric and Family Care, Advanced Laparoscopic Surgical Associates, Garden State Urology, Minute Clinic, Hackensack University Medical Center, and a Private Practice with Cedar Medical. She has also served as a legal reviewer, and has served as a Clinical Preceptor for Nurse Practitioner students at St. Peters College, Felician University, Ramapo College, and William Paterson University.



Mrs. Hearle earned both her Master of Science and Bachelor of Science in Nursing from Felician College located in Lodi, N.J. She completed her Associate Degree of Applied Science in Nursing from Bergen Community College located in Paramus, N.J.



Mrs. Hearle is a member of the American Academy of Nurse Practitioners, the Sigma Theta Tau Mu Theta Chapter, and SUNA, the Society of Urologic Nurses and Associates. She is also a recipient of the Professional Nurse Traineeship Award.



Mrs. Hearle takes pride in caring for her patients. She treats her patients the way she would like herself and her family to be treated.



Mrs. Hearle is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Michele  Doyle, NP
Bio: Family Medicine

Name: Jasmine  Lopez, OT
Bio: Occupational Therapy

Name: Melissa  Wixon, NP
Bio:
Melissa Wixon is a board-certified Family Nurse Practitioner through the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC). She brings more than 20 years of clinical experience to Middletown Medical, with a strong background in cardiology and bedside nursing.

Prior to becoming a Nurse Practitioner, Melissa spent over 18 years in cardiology at Garnet Health Medical Center and, before that, two years as a medical-surgical nurse at Arden Hill Hospital. Throughout her career, she has held multiple leadership and mentorship roles, including charge nurse, preceptor for new staff, and Chair of the Magnet Committee at Garnet—where she helped lead the facility to achieve Magnet Certification.

Melissa is also a Certified Medical-Surgical Registered Nurse (CMSRN) and maintains active BLS and ACLS certifications. In addition, she has served as an adjunct clinical instructor at SUNY Orange, teaching and mentoring future nurses.

Her academic journey began with an Associate Degree in Nursing from SUNY Orange. She went on to earn dual bachelor's degrees in nursing and business management, followed by a master's in nursing, and most recently, a Master of Science in Nursing with a Family Nurse Practitioner concentration from Utica University in 2023.

Melissa has been recognized with numerous awards and nominations throughout her career, including Daisy Award nominations, “Good Catch” awards for advancing patient safety, and Shining Star recognition for excellence in patient care. She is an active member of both the Nurse Practitioner Association of New York State and the American Association of Nurse Practitioners.

Melissa will be seeing patients in the Cardiology Department at Middletown Medical.

Name: Jennifer  Rivas, PhD, FNP-C
Bio:
Dr. Jennifer Rivas, PhD, FNP-C is a nomadic New Yorker.  She received her undergraduate degree in Psychology and later attended Farmingdale State College for Nursing School. She received her master’s degree at SUNY Upstate Medical College in their Family Nurse Practitioner program with a minor in Nursing Education.



She began her NP career at Weill Cornell Medical College where she collaborated with renowned neuro-oncologist and researcher, Dr. Howard Fine to establish a neuro-oncology clinical and clinical trial program. This is where she found her passion for research, pursuing a doctoral degree at Vanderbilt University. She graduated with her PhD in nursing science, health services research in 2023. Jennifer has ten plus years of experience, also having worked with Thomas Eanelli as a radiation oncology NP, been a part of the GHMC leadership team, and with the Department of Health specializing in home care services. 



Jennifer resides in the community and spends most of her free time rearing two small children and planning day trips with her family. She enjoys yoga and gardening.

Name: Nicole  Scancarello, PTA
Bio:
Nicole Scancarello, PTA is a devoted physical therapy professional serving patients at Middletown Medical Physical Therapy. She began her experience with Middletown Medical in 2020 as a Physical Therapist Assistant Student and joined the team full time after earning her Associate of Applied Science in Physical Therapist Assisting from SUNY Orange in Middletown, N.Y.



Nicole is also a certified Specialist in Strength and Conditioning by the International Sports Science Association and certified in Blood Flow Restriction Training.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Nicole served patients within Minisink Valley Central School District in Minisink, N.Y.; The Valley View Center for Nursing Care and Rehabilitation in Goshen, N.Y.; and Laura Stevens Physical Therapy in Highland Mills, N.Y.

Name: Robert  Hamilton, PMH-NP
Bio:
Robert Hamilton is a dual board-certified Nurse Practitioner in both Family Practice and Psychiatric Mental Health. He earned his Master of Science in Nursing as a Family Nurse Practitioner from Dominican College in 2016, followed by a Post-Master’s Certificate in Psychiatric Mental Health from SUNY Binghamton in 2018.



Robert brings a diverse clinical background that includes Urgent Care, Psychiatric Emergency Services, Adult Inpatient Psychiatry, and outpatient mental health treatment for both children and adults, ages 6 to 99. His approach is rooted in thoughtful diagnosis and personalized care, occasionally incorporating neuropsychological testing to fine-tune diagnoses when needed.

He has extensive experience treating conditions such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, ADHD (with a preference for non-stimulant treatments in adults), and behavioral issues associated with autism and dementia. For children and adolescents, he provides compassionate care for anxiety, OCD, ADHD, tic disorders, depression, and autism-related behaviors.

Robert is also trained in Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS), a non-invasive and well-tolerated option for treatment-resistant depression, especially in cases where traditional medications have fallen short.



Outside of work, Robert enjoys indoor and outdoor gardening, experimenting in the kitchen (and convincing his kids to try the results), discovering new music on Spotify, and following New York sports, especially the Knicks, Yankees, and Giants. He’s also a Minecraft mining partner to his daughters and stays up to date on emerging treatments in mental health.

Name: Bonnie  Seecharran, MD
Bio:
Cardiologist Dr. Seecharran received his medical degree from the United Medical and Dental Schools of Guy’s and St.Thomas’s Hospitals in London, England. Following graduation, he remained in England to complete his internship and residency in Internal Medicine. Dr.Seecharran’s post-graduate training continued at the University of Connecticut School of Medicine, where he served as Resident of Internal Medicine before his appointment as Fellow in Cardiology.



A contributor to several medical journal publications, he specializes in nuclear cardiology, transesophageal echocardiography, exercise stress testing, diagnostic cardiac catheterizations and many other procedural skills. With his practice of invasive non-interventional cardiology, Dr.Seecharran brings excellence to Middletown Medical, P.C.



He is the Director of the Cardiac Rehabilitation Program at Orange Regional Medical Center and an active Board member for the American Heart Association. His expertise and patient care are of the highest caliber.

Outside work, Dr.Seecharran pursues his interests in classical literature, art and tennis.

Name: Tisa  Saha, M.D, FACC, CBNC, CBCCT, RPVI
Bio:
Introducing Dr. Tisa Saha, an exceptional and highly accomplished cardiologist at Middletown Medical, PC's renowned Cardiology & Vascular Division. Dr. Saha's extensive credentials and expertise make her a sought-after specialist in the field. 



With an impressive list of board certifications, including Cardiovascular Diseases, adult echocardiography, cardiac coronary computer tomography, nuclear cardiology, and vascular ultrasound interpretation, Dr. Saha possesses a comprehensive understanding of various cardiac conditions and cutting-edge diagnostic techniques. Her commitment to maintaining dual licensure in Missouri and New York State showcases her dedication to providing exceptional care across different regions.



Dr. Saha's educational journey began at the esteemed Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland, where she completed her medical degree with honors distinction. Following her degree, she honed her skills through an additional year of internship before relocating to the United States to commence her residency. At the prestigious Pennsylvania Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania, she completed her residency, followed by specialized training in Cardiovascular Diseases at the renowned Donald and Barbara Zucker School of Medicine/Northwell (Lenox Hill Hospital) in the heart of New York City's Upper East Side.



Before joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Saha held the esteemed position of Director of Nuclear Cardiology and Assistant Professor in the Department of Cardiovascular Disease at the University of Missouri Kansas City (UMKC). Her tenure at UMKC reflects her commitment to advancing cardiac care through clinical excellence and academic contributions.



As a Fellow of the American College of Cardiology (FACC), Dr. Saha stands at the forefront of her field, embodying the highest standards of professional excellence and patient care. She is also an active member of the American Society of Echocardiography. Her previous affiliations with esteemed organizations such as the American College of Physicians, the American Medical Association, the Irish Medical Organization, the American Medical Student Association, the Cardiovascular Institute of Philadelphia, and Complex Cardiovascular Catheter Therapeutics further demonstrate her dedication to collaborative research and the advancement of cardiovascular medicine.



Dr. Saha's expertise has been widely recognized, and her contributions to medical literature are evident through her numerous peer-reviewed journal articles and abstracts. A complete list of her publications can be provided upon request, showcasing her commitment to advancing medical knowledge and improving patient outcomes.



Beyond her impressive medical accomplishments, Dr. Saha's linguistic abilities are an asset in fostering effective communication with patients from diverse backgrounds. Fluent in English, Bangla, Hindi, and Urdu, she ensures a comfortable and inclusive healthcare experience for all.



Dr. Saha looks forward to welcoming new patients starting July 1, as she continues her mission to provide compassionate, world-class cardiac care. Contact our office today to schedule an appointment and experience the expertise and compassion that have made Dr. Tisa Saha a trusted name in cardiology.

Name: Ramsey  Abadir, MD
Bio: Dr. Abadir earned his medical degree and completed his internship at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine, followed by a Diagnostic Radiology residency at Montefiore Hospital and Medical Center. Specializing in Interventional Radiology, Dr. Abadir received specialty fellowship training at Columbia Presbyterian Hospital. He is board certified by the American Board of Radiology in Diagnostic Radiology, with a certificate of added qualification in Vascular & Interventional Radiology.

Name: Barry S. Hyman M.D., Orthopedic Surgeon
Bio:
Dr. Hyman attended Columbia University where he earned a B.A. in Music/Pre Med, Masters’ Degrees in Physical Education and Education and his Doctorate in Science Education. He went on to graduate from Columbia’s College of Physicians and Surgeons where he received the New York Orthopedic Hospital Award.



Dr. Hyman completed his Orthopedic Residency at the Robert Wood Johnson Medical School, and his fellowship in Sports Medicine Orthopedic Surgery at the Johns Hopkins University. 



A lifelong passion for sports and the arts fostered Dr. Hyman’s interest in Orthopedics. He holds a 4th degree black belt in Judo, and is a former NY State Champion and Empire State Games Gold Medalist, and he swam and played water polo for Columbia University. He was the Head Judo Coach for Columbia, Princeton, and Yale Universities and has taught judo at West Point. 



Dr Hyman is Team physician for The Hudson Valley Renegades, and Wallkill High School. He has assisted the Team Physician for the Baltimore Orioles. 



Musically, Dr. Hyman is an accomplished cellist, and has played as a member of the Greater Newburgh Symphony Orchestra. Before coming to Orange Country he practiced in Connecticut and taught at Yale University where he helped develop a Performing Arts Medicine Program.



Clinically, Dr. Hyman’s interests are in injuries of the knee and shoulder and he is a Board Certified Orthopedic Surgeon. He is a member of the American Academy of Orthopedic Surgeons and the Arthroscopy Association of North America and served as the Chairman of the Department of Orthopedic Surgery at St. Luke’s Cornwall Hospital. Dr. Hyman is proud to continue his commitment to the community as an Orthopedic Surgeon, now as a member of the Middletown Medical team of medical professionals.

Name: Samantha  Garigliano, PA
Bio:
Samantha Garigliano joins Middletown Medical as a board-certified Physician Assistant. She is a recent graduate of Mercy College in Bronx, N.Y., where she received her Master’s Degree in Physician Assistant Studies. Through the program, Samantha Garigliano completed rotations in nine different areas of medicine, including Emergency Medicine, Surgery, Primary Care, Pediatrics, Internal Medicine, Dermatology, OB/GYN, Psychiatry, and Geriatrics.



Prior to her time at Mercy, Ms. Samantha Garigliano attended Auburn University in Auburn, Ala., where she graduated with her Bachelor of Science Degree in Microbiology. She is also BLS/ACLS certified, and is proficient in conversational Spanish.



Samantha Garigliano will serve patients through Middletown Medical’s Primary Care services and Urgent Care Center. She looks forward to helping her patients feel better, and stay as healthy as possible.

Name: David  Schwalb, MD
Bio:
Dr. David Schwalb has been with Middletown Medical’s Liberty Medical Group since 1991, and is a proud Liberty native.



Board Certified in Internal Medicine, Dr. Schwalb returned home to join the practice when it was run by his father, the late Dr. Alan Schwalb, and Dr. Donald Roth, who retired in 2018.



Previously, Dr. Schwalb was an Emergency Room Attending Physician with Mount Sinai Hospital in New York City, where he also served his Internship in Internal Medicine, followed by his Residency in Internal Medicine, and with concentrations on Emergency Room Medicine, Intensive Care Medicine and Cardiac Critical Care.  In addition, he spent several years as an Instructor in Clinical Medicine with the Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons in New York City.



Dr. Schwalb is a graduate of the Baylor College of Medicine in Houston, and earned an undergraduate degree with Summa Cum Laude honors from Hunter College in New York City.  He is also a Liberty High School graduate.

Name: Franklin  Guneratne, MD
Bio: Primary Care

Name: Imad  Abushahin, MD
Bio:
Dr. Imad Abushahin is a dedicated and patient-focused Orthopedic Surgeon who specializes in the treatment of the hand and upper extremity. 



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Abushahin was a Sports Medicine Fellow at the Cincinnati Sports Medicine and Orthopedic Center (CSMOC) in Cincinnati, Ohio in 2021-2022.



He also finished two years of clinical orthopedic fellowships in Hand and upper extremity. And one year of orthopedic trauma.



Dr. Abushahin has worked as:

• Assistant consultant in King Faisal Specialty Hospital and Research Center in Jeddah Saudi Arabia.

• Orthopedic consultant at the Specialty Hospital in Jordan., and as an orthopedic fellow at the King Hussein Cancer Center in Jordan, 

• Orthopedic resident at Jordan University Hospital in Jordan.



Dr. Abushahin earned his medical degree from the University of Jordan where he completed his Orthopedic Residency at its hospital.



Dr Abushahin main interest is Hand surgery, and he has developed his solid knowledge in this field by completing two years of clinical hand surgery training at Christine M. Kleinert Institute in Louisville, Kentucky and Cleveland Clinic Combined Fellowship in Ohio. He also has focused on Upper Extremity injuries as he completed a third year in Orthopedic Trauma at the University of Louisville Hospital in Kentucky.



He also spent last year in Cincinnati doing Sports Medicine fellowship at the Cincinnati Sports Medicine and Orthopedic Center (CSMOC).



Dr. Abushahin is a member of:

• Orthopedic Trauma Association (OTA).

• American Society for Surgery of the Hand (ASSH).

• American orthopedic society for Sport Medicine (AOSSM). 

• Jordanian Orthopedic Surgeon Association, Jordan Medical Council. He is Jordanian Board Certified in Orthopedic surgery. He is fully licensed to practice medicine in Ny, Ohio and Kentucky states.



Dr. Abushahin is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Kanayo  Eze, NP
Bio: Hospitalist

Name: Anthony  Martini, MD
Bio:
Dr. Martini is a Board Certified Internal Medicine physician with more than 30 years of quality care experience. He’s been operating his own private practice in Warwick since 2001, and will continue to do so in association with Middletown Medical.



A graduate of the Georgetown University School of Medicine, Dr. Martini earned his Bachelor’s Degree in Biology from the same University. He then completed his Internship and Residency at Hartford Hospital in Connecticut, where he was also a Leonard Fellow. From there, he spent close to a decade in New Jersey with the Garden State Medical Group, and the Health Insurance Plan (HIP) of New Jersey, before coming to Warwick.



Board Certified by the American College of Physicians/ASIM in Internal Medicine, Dr. Martini is also affiliated with St. Anthony Community Hospital in Warwick, where he served as Chief of Medicine, and sits on the Diabetes Physician Advisory Council.
Certifications: Board-Certified by the American College of Physicians

Name: Lynn  Maxwell, OT
Bio: Occupational Therapy

Name: Ramesh  Damacharla, MD
Bio:
Dr. Ramesh Kumar Damacharla is a Board-certified Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology provider in Middletown Medical’s Pain Management & Rehabilitation Division. He is certified by the American Board of Anesthesiology. Dr. Damacharla is trained in advanced approaches to pain relief, while maintaining thoughtful and compassionate patient care and treatment plans.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Damacharla served at a private family practice in Hyderabad, India; the POS General Hospital in Port of Spain, Trinidad; Howard University Hospital in Washington, D.C.; and Montefiore St. Luke's Cornwall Hospital in Newburgh, N.Y.



Dr. Damacharla earned his graduate medical degree from Rangaraya Medical College in Kakinada, India. He completed his General Surgery internship at the University Medical Center in Las Vegas, NV; his Anesthesiology Residency at the Upstate Medical Center in Syracuse, N.Y.; and his Pain Management Fellowship at the Upstate Medical Center in Syracuse, N.Y.



In addition to his clinical expertise, Dr. Damacharla has also participated in the Medtronic’s Fellows Course, Advanced Bionics Course, and Advanced Neuromodulation Course.



Dr. Damacharla is a member of the American Society of Anesthesiology, the New York State Society of Anesthesiology, the International Anesthesia Research Society, the Society of Cardiovascular Anesthesia, the Society of Pediatric Anesthesia, and the American Society of Regional Anesthesia. He is also advanced cardiac life support (ACLS) certified.



In his free time, Dr. Damacharla enjoys tennis, chess, computers, and travel.



Dr. Damacharla is currently accepting new patients 18 years of age or older at our Middletown location.

Name: Matthew  Koennecke, PT, DPT
Bio:
Matthew Koennecke, PT, DPT is a dedicated physical therapy professional serving patients at Middletown Medical Physical Therapy.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Matthew served patients at Excel Physical Therapy located in Media, Penn.; Bryn Mawr Rehab Hospital located in Malvern, Penn.; Kessler Institute for Rehabilitation Center located in Ramsey, N.J.; Physical Therapy and Wellness Institute located in Lansdale, Penn.; and Achieve Rehab and Nursing Facility located in Liberty, N.Y.



Matthew has also served patients at Middletown Medical during his time as a Physical Therapist Assistant from July 2015 through August 2019.



Matthew earned his Doctor of Physical Therapy from Widener University in Chester, Penn.; Master of Science Biomedical Sciences from Liberty University in Lynchburg, Va.; Bachelor of Arts in Biology from Dominican College in Blauvelt N.Y.; and Associates of Applied Science from SUNY Orange in Middletown, N.Y.



Matthew is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Jeffrey  Weinstein, MD
Locations: Monticello Center - Catskill Adult and Pediatric Medicine

Name: Sarah  Malik, PMHNP-BC
Bio:
Sarah Malik is a Board Certified Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner through the American Nurses Credentialing Center.



Sarah earned her Bachelor of Science in Nursing at Drexel University and went on to complete her Master of Science in Nursing with specialization in Psychiatry and Forensic Health at Binghamton University. 



Her educational journey was driven by a genuine passion for mental health care and a desire to contribute to her community. Having been born and raised in Orange Country, Sarah has always had a deep connection to our community and she is thrilled to return to provide compassionate and empathetic psychiatric care to her hometown.

Name: Dhirajlal  Shah, MD
Bio: Dr. Shah has met the health care needs of the Sullivan country community for over 30 years. He attended the Medical School at Makerere University in Kampala, Uganda and completed his Internal Medicine residency at Mount Vernon Hospital in Mount Vernon, NY. Dr. Shah has served thousands of patients with quality and compassionate care, which he continues to do with Middletown Medical. He emphasizes the human element of care to enhance his ability to successfully diagnose and treat even the most complicated patients. He is on staff at Orange Regional Medical Center in Middletown.

Name: Sharon  Valencia, DPM
Bio:
Dr. Valencia brings podiatry to Middletown Medical — our newest specialty medicine — with more than 20 years of compassionate experience in diagnosing and treating medical and surgical conditions of the lower extremity, including those resulting from bone and joint disorders, infectious diseases, muscular pathologies, as well as neurological, metabolic, and circulatory diseases.



Since 1996, Dr. Valencia has run a successful private practice in Goshen, performing examinations, diagnoses, and treatments, as well as office and outpatient surgical procedures on the lower extremity. In addition, she’s been a Consulting Podiatrist with Elant Inc. in Goshen (also since 1996), providing palliative care for several senior facilities, including nursing homes, adult homes and senior day care programs.



A graduate of the New York College of Podiatric Medicine, Dr. Valencia earned her undergraduate degree in Biology from The College of Mt. St. Vincent in New York. She completed a Surgical Residency with St. Mary Hospital in Hoboken, N.J.



Dr. Valencia is affiliated with Orange Regional Medical Center, and is also a tennis instructor, as well as a member of the National Athletic Trainer’s Association (NATA).

Name: Amy  Berretta, FNP-C , PMHNP - BC
Bio:
Amy is a board-certified Family Nurse Pracitioner ( FNP) and board-certified Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner (PMHNP).  She started her career in the medical field over 20 years ago and has grown to be a well-rounded provider.  She enjoys helping people and is well known to be  compassionate and caring .  She has enjoyed learning, growing and providing her skills that were obtained over the years to help people return to wellness. 



Expertise: 

Amy has 20 Years of healthcare field in various of settings.  She started her career in the medical field over 20 years ago as an LPN. Soon after she received her RN and worked in many nursing fields such as the Labor and Delivery , Rehabilitation, Cardiac floor and medical-surgical units in large NYC hospital.   Amy then received her Master's in FNP and began working in the Urgent care setting.  Soon after she found out she loved working in acute medicine and was a Hospitalist in a Large regional Hospital for 8 years.  Amy again then decided to return to school for her psychiatric certification which she received with Honors and began helping people in the outpatient setting with a various of mental health issues. 



Skills:

As being a bed side nurse for many years, Amy has developed a holistic way of treating medical and psychiatric ailments alongside traditional medicine and therapy. 

She specializes in treating the person as a "whole".  She is well studied in medication management in many fields and now her main focus is working in mental health.  



Education: 



SUNY Buffalo, Master Degree Graduate with Honors Post Masters Certification in PMHNP 2021

Dominican College, Master Degree Graduate with Honors Masters in FNP 2015. 

Chamberlain College of Nursing, Graduate with Honors IN Bachelors , Science in Nursing 2012 

Rockland Community College , Assos Degree in Nursing , Registered Nurse - 2006

Rockland Boces - Licensed Practical Nurse Certificate - 2003
Certifications: Specialty Certifications: Basic Life Support, Neonatal Resusitation Program, and S.T.A.B.L.E.

Name: Danielle  Belser, MD
Bio: Urgent Care

Name: Rajan  Gulati, MD
Bio:
Dr. Gulati began his private Family Practice in 1984. Through his vision of creating a large Immediate Care Center for the community, his private practice quickly expanded to become Middletown Medical, P.C.



A graduate of the Institution of Medical Science in Varanasi, India, he served his residencies in Psychiatry, Internal Medicine and Emergency Medicine at The University of Medicine and Dentistry of New Jersey. Dr. Gulati proudly served for a decade as Director of Horton Memorial Hospital’s Emergency Department and is a Fellow of the American Board of Emergency Physicians.



In his free time, he enjoys playing tennis and also practices the art of meditation.

Name: Ravi  Ramaswamy, MD
Bio:
Ravi Ramaswamy, M.D. is a board-certified nephrologist who has joined our team of healthcare providers. He is trained in all aspects of nephrology, including kidney disease and its causes. Dr. Ramaswamy is treating patients at 75 Maltese Drive in Middletown.



Dr. Ramaswamy completed his fellowship in nephrology in 2000 at the New York-Presbyterian/Weill Cornell Medical Center, and has treated patients through various positions at healthcare facilities throughout New York and India.



Dr. Ramaswamy received his initial training in India, earning his Bachelor of Science, Bachelor of Medicine, and Bachelor of Surgery from Mysore University. Dr. Ramaswamy, a diplomate of the American Board of Internal Medicine and the National Board of Examinations in India, is a member of the American College of Physicians, the American Society of Nephrology, and the American Association of Physicians of Indian Origin.

Name: Bridget  McKeever, PA-C
Bio:
Bridget McKeever is a physician assistant certified by the National Commission on Certification of Physician Assistants (NCCPA).



Prior to joining the Middletown Medical team, Ms. McKeever served patients with Access: Supports for Living in Middletown, N.Y. and Walden Recreational Department in Walden, N.Y.



While pursuing her certification as a physician assistant, Ms. McKeever gained a wealth of healthcare experience through her work with healthcare professionals at Ellenville Regional Hospital in Ellenville, N.Y.; Nephrology Associates of Syracuse in Syracuse, N.Y.; QuikMed in Watertown, N.Y.; Bright Futures Pediatrics in Liverpool. N.Y.; Family Practice Associates, PLLC in Liverpool, N.Y.; Strong Memorial Hospital in Rochester, N.Y.; St. Joseph’s Health in Syracuse, N.Y.; Upstate Addiction Center in Syracuse, N.Y.; and others.



Her diverse fields of study include emergency medicine, internal medicine, women’s health, urgent care, pediatrics, family medicine, cardiovascular medicine, surgery, and behavioral health.



Ms. McKeever earned her Bachelor of Arts in Biology from Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, N.Y. and her Master of Science in Physician Assistant Studies at Le Moyne College in Syracuse, N.Y.



Ms. McKeever holds certifications in advanced life support (ALS), basic life support, Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT), and “Stop The Bleed.” She has also been trained in infection control and child abuse and neglect/maltreatment identification.



She is a member of the New York State Society for Physician Assistants (NYSSPA) and the American Academy of Physician Assistants (AAPA). Ms. McKeever is a certified Emergency Medical Technician (EMT).



She is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Natalia  Perry, FNP-BC
Bio:
Natalia Perry is a vibrant and compassionate Dermatology Nurse Practitioner who brings both heart and expertise to the world of skin health and aesthetics. With a dual passion for medical dermatology and helping people look and feel their best, Natalia blends science and artistry in every patient encounter. 

She earned both her Bachelor and Master of Science in Nursing from Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, NY. Before diving into dermatology, Natalia spent eight adrenaline-filled years as an Emergency Department RN at Columbia University Medical Center. There, she sharpened her critical thinking, tackled high-pressure situations with grace, and earned her Certified Emergency Nurse (CEN) credentials, all while cultivating a calm, confident bedside manner. She also hit the road for two years as a local travel nurse, expanding her clinical expertise and adaptability. 

Natalia’s love for dermatology sparked during her graduate clinical rotations, where she discovered how transformative skin care can be—both medically and emotionally. Since then, she’s made it her mission to help patients achieve healthy, glowing skin and feel confident in their own skin. 

When she’s not in the clinic, you can find Natalia planning her next travel adventure, staying active (preferably outdoors!), or hunting down the best foodie spots in town. Her approachable energy, clinical know-how, and genuine love for her work make her a favorite among patients seeking both results and a relatable, trusted provider.

Name: Vijayalakshmi  Rampam, MD
Bio:
Dr. Vijayalakshmi Rampam is a dedicated and patient-focused Family Medicine physician.



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Rampam served patients with Family Medical Care, PC in Newburgh, N.Y. She has also served as an attending physician at Garnet Health Medical Center in Middletown, N.Y.; as House Physician in the Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology at Lutheran Medical Center in Brooklyn, N.Y.; within the Department of Cardiology at Fallston General Hospital in Fallston, Maryland; and within the Department of Primary Care and Psychology at the VA Hospital in Washington, D.C.



Dr. Rampam has also spent time serving Middletown Medical patients at the Middletown, N.Y. Urgent Care Center prior to now joining the practice full-time.



Dr. Rampam earned her Doctor of Medicine from Sri Venkateswara University Medical College in Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh, India. She completed her continuing medical education program with the National Medical School Review in Newark, New Jersey. Her residency in Family practice was completed at Lutheran Medical Center in Brooklyn, N.Y.



Dr. Rampam is a member of the American Medical Association and is a Member of American Academy of Family Physicians.



Dr. Rampam is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Lori  Musorrafiti, FNP-BC
Bio:
Lori Musorrafiti, FNP-BC is a family nurse practitioner with over 10 years’ of experience in the medical field. She is board-certified by the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC).



Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Ms. Musorrafiti served at a number of notable healthcare facilities across New York State, including Orange Medical Care in Newburgh, N.Y., Sandra Sadler Pediatrics in Haverstraw, N.Y., Nazario’s Practice in Middletown, N.Y., Southern Westchester Diabetes in the Bronx, N.Y., and Hudson Pointe at Riverdale Center in the Bronx, N.Y. Prior to becoming a nurse practitioner, she also served as a sexual assault forensic examiner at the Center for Safety and Change in Rockland Country, N.Y. and as a Registered Nurse (RN) at JCCA in Pleasantville, N.Y.



Her specialized experience has included Endocrinology, Emergency Medicine, Addiction Medicine, Primary Care, Gynecology, and more.



Ms. Musorrafiti earned her Bachelor of Arts in Communications from Marist College in Poughkeepsie, N.Y. and her Bachelor of Science in Nursing from Dominican College in Blauvelt, N.Y. She earned her Master of Science in Nursing/Family Nurse Practitioner from Dominican College in Blauvelt, N.Y.



Ms. Musorrafiti also holds a Buprenorphine provider certification and is a New York Sexual Assault Forensic Examiner, certified by the New York State Department of Health.



She holds specialized experience in the treatment of opioid use disorder, including dispensing the prescription Suboxone, one of the main medications used to treat opioid addiction. She also provides education and counseling related to opioid use disorder and pain medication management.



Ms. Musorrafiti is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Matthew  Staback, PT, DPT, ATC, AIB-VRC
Bio:
Matthew J. Staback, PT, DPT, ATC, AIB-VRC is a dedicated physical therapy professional who holds dual licensure as a Doctor of Physical Therapy and as an Athletic Trainer.

 

Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Matthew served as a certified and licensed Athletic Trainer with JAG-ONE Physical Therapy in Sleepy Hollow, NY and was also a member of the Atlantic Coast Conference (ACC) medical staff during the Men’s NCAA Division 1 conference basketball tournament in Brooklyn, NY in 2018. Matthew has experience working with athletes ranging from adolescents to those at the collegiate level and beyond.

 

Matthew earned his Bachelor of Science in Athletic Training from Marywood University in Scranton, PA and his Doctorate of Physical Therapy from Mercy College in Dobbs Ferry, NY where he graduated with Distinction. He holds previous clinical experience treating patients with a variety of orthopedic and neurological conditions at St. Mary Rehabilitation Hospital in Langhorne, PA; Good Samaritan Hospital in Suffern, NY; and Joint Movements Physical Therapy in West Haverstraw, NY.

 

Matthew also previously served patients with Middletown Medical, PC during his graduate clinical experience in 2021.

 

Matthew is certified in Vestibular and Concussion Rehabilitation through the American Institute of Balance.

 

Matthew is currently accepting new patients.

Name: Douglas K. Osofsky, DPM
Bio:
Douglas K. Osofsky, D.P.M. brings over 30 years of experience to the Podiatry team at Middletown Medical. As a board-certified physician and surgeon in podiatric medicine, he specializes in the medical diagnosis and treatment of foot and ankle problems, including orthopedic and dermatological conditions resulting from bone and joint, neurological, and circulatory issues.



Dr. Osofsky is a graduate of the New York College of Podiatric Medicine, and earned his Bachelor of Science in Biology from SUNY Geneseo. He completed his internships with Coney Island Hospital and Baptist Medical Center, both in Brooklyn, N.Y.



Dr. Osofsky is affiliated with Orange Regional Medical Center in Middletown, N.Y., and is licensed by the State of New York. He is married, and resides in Fishkill, N.Y.

Name: Jagruti P Gohel, MD
Bio:
Dr. Gohel completed a Family Medicine residency at Guthrie Robert Packer Hospital in Sayre, Pennsylvania after earning her degree in medicine and surgery from M.P. Shah Medical College in India. She worked at the Heart Center of New Jersey, specializing in outpatient cardiac treatments. She also participated in research studies of glycemic optimization and glucose control for diabetic patients.



Dr. Gohel is well trained in all aspects of family medicine, with attention to her patients’ physical and emotional well being. She believes that quality care is best achieved through strong patient relationships and a focus on prevention. Dr. Gohel is a member of the American Academy of Family Physicians.

Name: John  Podeszwa, MD
Bio:
After receiving his medical degree from SUNY Downstate Medical Center in Brooklyn, NY, Dr. Podeszwa completed his residency at Staten Island University Hospital. He served as fellow of Gastroenterology at The Brooklyn Hospital-Caledonian Hospital. Dr. Podeszwa spent the past decade in private practice, prior to joining Middletown Medical, P.C. He is highly skilled in a multitude of specialized procedures, including upper endoscopy, colonoscopy, polypectomy.

During his off hours, Dr. Podeszwa enjoys woodworking, scuba diving and skiing. He and his wife, Jeanne, are the parents of two children.

Name: Cynthia  Delavalle, MS, AGPCNP-BC
Bio:
Cynthia DeLavalle, MS, AGPCNP-BC is an adult-gerontology nurse practitioner board-certified by the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC).  



Prior to her current role, Ms. DeLavalle worked locally treating patients in the specialty of Interventional Pain Management at the North American Partners in Pain Management in Poughkeepsie, N.Y. for the past four years. 



Ms. DeLavalle began her career in health care over 30 years ago as a registered nurse, working in acute care settings.  While working as a Registered nurse, she advanced her skills to work in adult critical care and Cardio-Thoracic critical care settings.  She then pursued her interest in nursing, graduating with honors, and received the Sr. Leona DeBoer Graduate Nursing Award at Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, NY.  Additionally, Ms. DeLavalle served as an adjunct professor with the nursing department at Mount Saint Mary College in Newburgh, N.Y. 



Ms. DeLavalle has been an active member of several professional organizations including the American Nurses Association, American Association of Critical Care Nurses, and was inducted into the Honor Society of Nursing, Sigma theta Tau International in 2015.  She also holds current and past certifications in basic life support (BLS), Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS), Pediatric Advanced Life Support (PALS), and Adult Critical Care Certification (CCRN). 



She is active with Saint Mary’s Church in Washingtonville, N.Y., has participated in various community fundraising and events, is a Charter member of the Washingtonville Kiwanis Club (2015), and is an affiliate member of the Chelsea Yacht Club since 2019.   



Outside the office, Ms. DeLavalle enjoys spending time with her family, supporting community events, and outdoor activities, including racing sailboats, bicycling, swimming, hiking, skiing, and show-shoeing.

Name: Christopher  Matthews, DNP FNP-C
Bio:
Christopher Matthews is a board-certified family nurse practitioner with a strong background in acute patient care. He is working with Sullivan Internal Medicine Group in Monticello (SIMG)



Mr. Matthews has served patients as a registered nurse at West Houston Medical Center in Houston, Texas, the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston, Texas, and Lourdes Hospital in Binghamton, N.Y.



Mr. Matthews is a graduate of Binghamton University, where he earned his Bachelor of Science in nursing and his Master of Science in advanced family nursing. He also completed the Family Health Nurse Practitioner Certificate Program, designed for nurses seeking advanced clinical preparation.



Mr. Mathews is certified by the American Academy of Nurse Practitioners. Additionally, he has earned Trauma Nurse Core Course, Emergency Nurse Pediatric Course, Advanced and Pediatric Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support, and Basic Life Support certifications while in practice for becoming a registered nurse.
Certifications: Board-Certified Family Nurse Practitioner by the American Academy of Nurse Practitioners
Advanced and Pediatric Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support
Locations: Monticello Center - Sullivan Internal Medicine Group

Name: Ayman  Matta, MD FACS
Bio:
Ayman Matta, M.D., F.A.C.S. is Board Certified by the American Board of Ophthalmology and is a Fellow of the American College of Surgeons. Dr. Matta specializes in Cataract Surgery, Refractive Surgery, Glaucoma, Corneal Transplants, Medical and Laser Retina Surgery, including LASIK, PRK, PTK , AK , and Lens Exchange Therapy.

Dr. Matta completed medical school and his internship at Ain Shams University School of Medicine, and completed his residency at Albert Einstein College of Medicine at the Catholic Medical Center of Brooklyn and Queens and the State University of New York, Stony Brook at the Nassau University Medical Center. He then went on to complete a fellowship in Anterior Segment and Cornea & Refraction at the Bucci Cataract and Laser Vision Institute in Pennsylvania.

Name: Donna  Collins, PA
Bio: Donna received both her Bachelor of Science degree and Master of Science degree in Physician Assistant Studies from DeSales University in Center Valley, PA. Through the program, she completed nine rotations in different areas of medicine, including Internal and Family Medicine, Pediatrics, OB/GYN, Emergency Medicine and Urgent Care. During that time, she worked with numerous practitioners in private practice and hospital settings. She is a member of the American Academy of Physician Assistant.

Name: Ruth  Demetros, DNP FNP BC.
Bio:
Ruth Demetros, D.N.P., F.N.P.-B.C. is a licensed

family nurse practitioner who has spent her entire

professional career in the medical eld, both as a

nurse practitioner and as a registered nurse. She is

board-certied by the American Nurses Credentialing

Center (ANCC).

Ms. Demetros has experience in a variety of practice

areas, including emergency medicine, intensive

care, progressive care, and coronary care.

Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Ms. Demetros

most recently worked as a registered nurse at St.

Francis Hospital (formerly known as Mid-Hudson

Regional Hospital of Westchester Medical Center)

in Poughkeepsie, N.Y. Prior to becoming a registered

nurse, she also served as a patient care technician

at St. Francis Hospital.

Ms. Demetros earned both her Doctor of Nursing

Practice and her Bachelor of Science in Nursing at

Liberty University in Lynchburg, Va. She earned her

Associate of Science in Nursing at Dutchess Community

College in Poughkeepsie, N.Y.

Ms. Demetros is currently welcoming new patients.

Name: Tamara  Baldesweiler, ANP
Bio:
Tamara Baldesweiler is a board-certified adult nurse practitioner providing care to Middletown Medical’s patients at Orange Regional Medical Center. She provides proper diagnoses and treatments for a wide range of medical conditions. Ms. Baldesweiler focuses on disease management and wellness maintenance specifically in the adult population.



Ms. Baldesweiler has 10 years of experience in the field of emergency trauma and specialty care transport, which she gained as a registered nurse with The Valley Hospital in Ridgewood, N.J., Hackensack University Medical Center in Hackensack, N.J., American Mobile Healthcare (serving hospitals in New York and New Jersey), and Good Samaritan Hospital in Suffern, N.Y.



She is also an EMS provider for Greenwood Lake Volunteer Ambulance Corps in Greenwood Lake, N.Y., and a ski patroller with the National Ski Patrol in Sterling Forest, N.Y.



Ms. Baldesweiler earned her Bachelor of Science in nursing from Pace University and her Master of Science in nursing (concentration: adult/gerontology) from Fairleigh Dickinson University.

Name: Melanye  Rockwell, FNP
Bio:
Melanye Rockwell is a nationally certified Family Nurse Practitioner (FNP) with a decade of experience in rural medicine, including the past four years in advanced practice. Her background spans acute care, family medicine, cardiology, and school nursing, giving her a deep connection to the communities she serves and the ability to care for both healthy and chronically ill patients.



Melanye has extensive expertise in developmental and neurodivergent medical care needs. She is trained in addiction medicine, worker’s compensation, medical cannabis, and the Federal Motor Carrier requirements for DOT physicals. Her academic background includes undergraduate degrees from SUNY Morrisville and Hartwick College, and a graduate degree from SUNY Binghamton University, where she was recognized as a HRSA/ANEW scholar and awardee.  She also completed a one-year Family Nurse Practitioner Residency, funded by HRSA, through Bassett Medical Center with a focus on rural family medicine.



Certified by the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC), Melanye is a committed educator and mentor. She has precepted and taught student nurses, student nurse practitioners, and resident nurse practitioners, guiding them in their transition into independent practice. For the past two years, she has served as a certified Patient-Centered Medical Home provider, helping patients achieve improved health outcomes, a mission she looks forward to continuing with Middletown Medical.



Outside of her professional life, Melanye is a proud mother of three daughters. Together, they enjoy hiking, swimming, kayaking, and thrift store shopping. She lives by the motto: “Just because it’s the way it has always been done doesn’t mean it’s the right way,” which fuels her commitment to advocating for safe and effective patient care.

Name: Alicia  Natalizio, FNP-BC
Bio:
Alicia Natalizio, MSN, FNP-BC is a board-certified Family Nurse Practitioner specializing in medical and cosmetic dermatology. With a passion for helping patients achieve healthy, confident skin, Alicia offers personalized care for a wide range of conditions including acne, eczema, psoriasis, and skin cancer screenings, as well as cosmetic treatments such as injectable therapies and customized chemical peels. 



Alicia began her healthcare journey at Mountainside School of Nursing, where she graduated in 2014. She spent the next eight years building a strong clinical foundation as a bedside nurse, developing a deep understanding of patient care and complex medical conditions.



 Her desire to advance her impact led her to earn her Master of Science in Nursing (MSN) from Walden University in 2022. Known for her warm, thorough approach, Alicia blends evidence-based practice with a keen eye for aesthetic detail.



When she’s not in the office helping patients feel confident in their skin, she’s likely planning her next weekend escape, exploring new towns, or tracking down the best coffee shop. She loves wandering charming main streets, collecting skincare favorites, and redesigning her space to feel fresh and inviting. Whether she’s refreshing a skincare routine, a room, or a travel itinerary, she brings the same love for details, warmth, and a little glow wherever she goes.

Name: Murray  Schwalb, MD
Bio:
Murray David Schwalb, M.D.

UROLOGY



Dr. Murray Schwalb is a practicing urologist certified by the American Board of Urology. He is licensed to practice in New York and New Jersey.

 

In addition to his current position, Dr. Schwalb also serves as a clinical associate professor of urology at Touro College of Osteopathic Medicine in New York, N.Y. as well as at New York Medical College in New York, N.Y. Prior to joining Middletown Medical, Dr. Schwalb served as the chief of Urology in the Department of Surgery at St. Barnabas Hospital as well as Lincoln Hospital, both in the Bronx, N.Y. He was also a partner at Orange Country Urological Associates in Middletown, N.Y.



Dr. Schwalb holds affiliations with St. Anthony Community Hospital in Warwick, N.Y., Fishkill Ambulatory Surgery Center in Fishkill, N.Y., St. Barnabas Hospital in the Bronx, N.Y., Westchester Medical Center in Valhalla, N.Y., and Lincoln Hospital in the Bronx, N.Y.



Dr. Schwalb earned his Doctor of Medicine from New York Medical College in Valhalla, N.Y. He earned his Bachelor of Arts degree at Yeshiva University in New York, N.Y. He completed residencies in both urology and surgery. He also completed an advanced Urologic oncology fellowship at Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center in New York, N.Y.



Dr. Schwalb is a member of the American Urological Association and a fellow of the American College of Surgeons.

Name: Heather  Wilantewicz, PA
Bio: Primary Care

Name: Jonathan  Freet, Physician Assistant
Bio: Currently Working in Middletown Urgent Care Bio Coming Soon

Name: Bikramjit  Singh, M.D.
Bio:
Dr. Bikramjit Singh is an experienced hospitalist currently treating patients at Orange Regional Medical Center in Middletown, N.Y. He is the medical director of Middletown Medical’s Hospitalist Program and an adjunct clinical professor at Touro College of Osteopathic Medicine in Middletown, N.Y. He also serves as a preceptor – a practicing physician who volunteers to give personal instruction, training, and supervision to medical students – at Orange Regional Medical Center.

Certified by both the American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM) and the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG), he is licensed to practice in New York State, New Jersey State, and Pennsylvania.
